name: Old Fashioned Peach-Gingerbread Upside-Down Cake
recipeIngredient: 4 peaches (peeled, pitted, and sliced 1/4 inch thick)
8 peaches (peeled, pitted, and sliced 1/4 inch thick)
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees.
2. Generously butter a 12-inch springform pan. Arrange 4 of the sliced peaches in a circular pattern in the pan and set aside.
3. Sift flour and baking soda.
4. Combine molasses and boiling water.
5. In a mixing bowl, combine eggs and sugar and beat for 5 to 10 minutes until light and fluffy. Add the melted butter gradually while still beating. Alternately add the flour mixture and molasses mixture, mixing just until smooth.
6. Pour batter over peaches in the springform pan.
7. Place on the center rack in the preheated oven and bake for 45 minutes or until a toothpick inserted in the center comes out clean. Remove cake from the oven and let cool.
8. Saute the remaining peach slices in the melted butter and brown sugar until soft but not mushy, about 6 to 8 minutes. Remove and let cool.
9. Invert the cake onto a serving platter so that the peaches are on top. Arrange the cooled peach slices around the top of the cake.
10. Melt the preserves in a double boiler and generously brush over the sliced peaches.
11. This dessert is extremely elegant and perfect for company.
